results.reason: There is no perimeter at all; four buildings float in an unbounded flat green field -- the exact open-field failure the grammar forbids.
results.title: Bounded perimeter

results.reason: Paths form a single rigid plus-shaped cross of uniform width joining building centres, with no branching or curvature.
results.title: Organic branching paths

results.reason: There is no compression/release and no bends, and the path arms dead-end near building walls instead of meeting the doors.
results.title: Path rhythm and door alignment

results.reason: All four buildings are the same brown shell at near-identical footprint, differentiated only by a sign -- one repeated shell.
results.title: Varied building massing

results.reason: A single brown timber material is used for every structure over flat green; there is no material identity or contrast.
results.title: Distinct material identity

results.reason: Decoration is a handful of isolated props (a log, a few flowers, rocks, buckets) in a mostly empty field, with no interior scatter or edge clustering.
results.title: Decoration density

results.reason: No landmark dominates; the eye finds four equally-weighted identical boxes and nothing specific to move toward.
results.title: Dominant landmark

results.reason: The first read is 'four boxes and a crossroad in a void,' not a recognisable kind of place.
results.title: Readable first view

results.reason: Event/reward positions are bare markers sitting in empty grass, not dressed clusters.
results.title: Dressed reward clusters

results.reason: There is no water feature and the only open space is the hard rectangular path intersection.
results.title: Water or organic plaza

results.reason: The material vocabulary is limited -- arguably too limited -- so this lone criterion does not fail, though for the wrong reason.
results.title: Limited coherent vocabulary

results.reason: Nothing is statable beyond 'generic four-box village'; there is no memorable identity.
results.title: Memorable identity
